texto: To lead a human life, a man must have a notion of himself as having a past and a future.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mary Warnock sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “To lead a human life, a man must have a notion of himself as having a past and a future.” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, memory, human, útil em momentos de reflexao.

texto: Without imagination we should be lost; for only with its help can we interpret our experience, turn it into experience of an outer world, and thus make use of it in understanding what and where we are, and what we need to do.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mary Warnock sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “Without imagination we should be lost; for only with its help can we interpret our experience, tu…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, imagination, perception, útil em momentos de perception.

frase_original: Without imagination we should be lost; for only with its help can we interpret our experience, turn it into experience of an outer world, and thus make use of it in understanding what and where we are, and what we need to do.

texto: Without imagination, we merely see or hear, and even if we see or hear that the objects of the senses are beautiful, we cannot feel that they are so. The difference is this: in feeling the beauty of objects, we enjoy not only the common, shared pleasures of the senses, but also the private pleasures of the imagination, peculiar to ourselves, and such that we have to struggle to articulate them.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mary Warnock sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Without imagination, we merely see or hear, and even if we see or hear that the objects of the se…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a felicidade, beauty, imagination, útil em momentos de imagination.

The difference is this: in feeling the beauty of objects, we enjoy not only the common, shared pleasures of the senses, but also the private pleasures of the imagination, peculiar to ourselves, and such that we have to struggle to articulate them.
